title: The Witness
setting: An optical reflection inside the deepest plane of the living lens itself. Midnight cyan and starlight.
theme: The player becoming aware of the Iris itself.
narrative_introduction: The instrument does not open into a room. It turns inward. You are looking through the cornea, through the stroma fibers, right into the pupil aperture itself. For the first time, notice who is looking back.

mechanics.observation: Locked-off 2-second cinematic moment. The living iris breathes, the pupil aperture dilates to reveal four orbiting memory shards from the previous moments, and the center reflects the observer's own gaze.
mechanics.memory: Spatial fragment placement inside the eye: unfinished canvas reflection, museum pocket watch shard, violin bow fiber, quartz sensor crystal, golden collarette ring, central pupil portal.
mechanics.discovery: Frozen moment exploration. Attuning to the four memory shards reveals that every moment you witnessed literally altered the physical stroma of the eye.
mechanics.evidence: Archive entry reveals the ultimate truth of Two Second Witness: the observer and the instrument are one unified consciousness.
mechanics.reflection: Iris voice synthesizes the completion of Chapter 1. Rank 2: Witness unlocks. Archive entry: 'The Witness'.

environment.location: internal stroma plane of the Living Iris, pupillary boundary

lighting.primary: bioluminescent seafoam and emerald limbal glow
lighting.fill: starlight reflections caught inside the transparent cornea glass
lighting.key_detail: four orbiting memory shards reflecting the exact lighting of WM_001 through WM_004

camera.style: intimate internal optical macro lens shot
camera.depth_of_field: infinite optical depth across all stroma layers
camera.color_grade: deep space cyan, rich golden collarette highlights, pristine crystal teal

animation.memory_shards: orbit smoothly in Keplerian ellipses around the central pupil aperture
animation.pupil_portal: dilates slightly to show deep infinite optical stars inside

ambient_details: golden collarette fiber ring glowing around the aperture
Memory Shard 1 displaying the golden prism from The Unfinished Canvas
Memory Shard 2 displaying the brass watch from The Forgotten Museum
Memory Shard 3 displaying the Stradivarius bow from The Last Performance
Memory Shard 4 displaying the quartz sensor from The Faulty Reactor
curved cornea surface reflecting the observer's own silhouette

audio.base_layer: deep 48 Hz Hippus sub-bass respiration, silent atmospheric vacuum
audio.focus_layer: crystalline 4.2 kHz optical resonance, soft organic stroma rustle
audio.transition: multi-octave Atmospheric Phase Lock chord C-G-E sustaining into infinity

surface: four memory shards orbit the pupil
subtle: golden collarette ring pulses exactly in time with your own respiration
memory shards cast faint colored reflections onto the teal fibers
deep: each memory shard rotates to show the exact detail you carried from that chapter
cornea glass reflects your own observer silhouette looking into the lens
intentionally_missed_first_view: pupil void contains an infinite spiral of future unwritten moments

reconstruction.iris_prompt: Reconstruct the Living Lens. Place the memories that shaped our perception.

attunements.reveals: light from the artist's loft permanently brightened the upper ciliary stroma fibers
warmth from the night guard's palm imprint remains preserved in the left collarette
dedication note from Bach's score vibrates in exact harmonic resonance with the right rim
precision quartz grid alignment permanently calibrated the pupil's optical focus

investigation.iris_intervention: You have witnessed all five horizons. The empty eye is empty no more. We have learned to see.

revelation.iris_response: You noticed the four memory shards orbiting the aperture. You noticed the golden collarette born from your attention. You noticed that every moment you carried became a physical part of this living eye. You did not solve five puzzles. You learned how to see. Rank 2: Witness unlocked.

archive_mapping.title: The Witness
archive_mapping.iris_note: The instrument and the observer looked into each other and discovered they were holding the same light.
